reasoning:
The forecast period is extremely short, with only 67 days remaining from the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26, 2025) to the resolution date (Jan 1, 2026). While Chegg is undeniably in severe financial distress—characterized by plummeting revenues, "enormous accounting losses," and significant job cuts (22% in May 2025)—several factors strongly suggest a bankruptcy filing will not occur before 2026. 

Crucially, Chegg's primary debt consists of 0% Convertible Notes maturing in 2026. This means the company is not burdened by near-term cash interest payments that might otherwise trigger a liquidity crisis in Q4 2025. Bankruptcies unprompted by sudden debt maturities or covenant breaches usually happen when operational cash drops to zero, but the major cost-cutting measure from May 2025 has likely extended their runway. Furthermore, third-party analysis from late 2024 explicitly projects Chegg's bankruptcy horizon to be between mid-2026 and late 2027 based on their quarterly cash burn. Without immediate debt maturities to force a filing before the end of the year, it is highly probable Chegg will try to weather the remainder of 2025 or negotiate out-of-court restructurings into early 2026.
batch_queries: Will Chegg file for bankruptcy before January 1, 2026?
